signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical Shock + Carry + Intervention Risk
signals.text: WTI Oil +5.63% at $92.3 following U.S.-Iran talk collapse and Strait of Hormuz blockade. USD/JPY at 159.68 remains supported by +300bp Fed-BoJ carry (Fed 3.75% vs BoJ 0.75%) and safe-haven USD demand. JPY COT LF net short -86,249 (31.6% long) indicates crowded carry positioning vulnerable to volatility, yet JPY safe-haven function remains suppressed while VIX holds at 15.8. 160.00 remains the key level for BoJ verbal warnings; intervention risk rises on a sustained break above this handle.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + Correlation Break
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5940 showing acute divergence as COT LF net short -13,767 (29.7% long) fades a hawkish RBNZ bias (market bias: Hike). NZD/USD vs NZX 50 correlation at +0.409 (z=+1.74σ) marks a significant break from the -0.212 norm, signaling NZD is decoupling from local equities to track global USD funding stress. If RBNZ signals a hike on 8 July, short-squeeze risk is elevated against crowded spec shorts.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Volatility + Retail Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7166 faces late-trend exhaustion as COT LF net long reaches +58,041 (76.1% long) while retail sentiment is 75% short—a classic institutional vs retail divergence. Options market reflects high anxiety with AUD/USD IV at 15.9% vs HV30 at 8.1%, creating a +7.8% Vol Risk Premium (VRP). Despite a -60bp carry deficit vs USD (RBA 4.35% vs Fed 3.75%), the extreme long positioning and high hedging costs suggest upside is structurally limited.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Volatility Premium + ECB Hike Bias
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1637 supported by ECB hike expectations for the 11 June meeting despite a -175bp carry deficit (Fed 3.75% vs ECB 2.00%). IV at 7.1% vs HV30 at 4.2% generates a +2.9% VRP, signaling the options market is pricing in protection for a hawkish ECB shift. Positive CESI (net +6) confirms Eurozone data is outperforming expectations, providing fundamental cover for the ECB to act as inflation risks rise.

signals.title: CHF/JPY — Retail Sentiment Extreme
signals.text: CHF/JPY at 203.026 testing retail exhaustion levels with 85% of retail traders net short. This extreme contrarian signal aligns with a -75bp carry disadvantage for CHF (SNB 0.0% vs BoJ 0.75%) and a net short COT position (LF net -5,651). Retail capitulation risk is high if JPY safe-haven demand fails to materialize despite geopolitical headlines, potentially forcing a squeeze toward the 205.00 handle.
